title: Both sides modified function parameters differently
description: One developer added a 'timeout' parameter while another added a 'retries' parameter to the same HTTP request function. The resolution must include both new parameters.
ours_description: Added timeout parameter with default value (PR #51: Request timeout support)
theirs_description: Added retries parameter with exponential backoff (PR #54: Retry logic)

key_elements.description: both timeout and retries in function signature

title: Variable rename on one side, new logic using old name on other
description: One developer renamed 'user_data' to 'profile' throughout the function for clarity, while another developer added new validation logic that references 'user_data'. The resolution must use the renamed variable consistently.
ours_description: Renamed user_data to profile for consistency (PR #88: Naming conventions)
theirs_description: Added age verification before account creation (PR #91: Age gate)
conflict_file:
<<<<<<< HEAD
function createAccount(profile) {
  validateEmail(profile.email);
  const account = db.create(profile);
  sendWelcomeEmail(profile.email);
  return account;
}
=======
function createAccount(user_data) {
  validateEmail(user_data.email);
  if (user_data.age < 18) {
    throw new Error('Must be 18 or older to create an account');
  }
  const account = db.create(user_data);
  sendWelcomeEmail(user_data.email);
  return account;
}
>>>>>>> feature/age-gate

key_elements.pattern: Must be 18 or older to create an account

title: Both sides modified a CSS class with different properties
description: One developer changed the button color scheme to match a rebrand, while another added responsive sizing. Both changes apply to the same selector.
ours_description: Updated button colors for rebrand (PR #102: Brand refresh)
theirs_description: Added responsive sizing for mobile (PR #105: Mobile responsiveness)

title: One side added error handling, other added a new code path
description: One developer wrapped the file parser in try/except for robustness, while another added support for YAML files alongside the existing JSON support. The resolution needs both error handling and YAML support.
ours_description: Added error handling around file parsing (PR #37: Graceful parse failures)
theirs_description: Added YAML support to config parser (PR #39: YAML config files)

title: Both sides modified a docker-compose service differently
description: One developer added health check configuration while another added volume mounts and environment variables to the same service. Both changes are needed for the complete service definition.
ours_description: Added health check for the web service (PR #112: Container health monitoring)
theirs_description: Added volume mounts and env vars for local dev (PR #115: Dev environment setup)
base_content:
services:
  web:
    image: myapp:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    depends_on:
      - db
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"

gold_resolution:
services:
  web:
    image: myapp:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8080/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
    volumes:
      - ./src:/app/src
      - ./config:/app/config
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://dev:dev@db:5432/myapp
      - DEBUG=true
    depends_on:
      - db
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
